title: beyeria cinerea subsp. cinerea
intro: Beyeria cinerea subsp. cinerea, also known as the spotted beyeria, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Cephalotaceae, traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, with herbal preparations also including the use of the plant's leaves and stems in a range of medicinal and therapeutic applications.
